title: Source lineage for The Brut, or The Chronicles of England: Brie's selected compilation
summary: The pinned CME XML is a repository version of U-M's 2006 APG1531 encoding, which encodes selected pages from Friedrich W. D. Brie's 1906/1908 two-volume edition. Brie combines the common prose Brut through 1333 with separately transmitted continuations, extracts, appendices, and poems. The selected content ends in 1475; the U-M header's 1479 claim is retained only as an attributed statement about a broader continuation tradition. Seventeen base-text relations, fifteen collations, complete and selected digital surrogates, and access-specific rights are kept as distinct layers.

entities.label: Brie's selected medieval textual contents through 1475
entities.description: Conceptual edition-level selection of medieval contents printed by Brie: the common prose Brut through 1333 and selected continuations, appendices, extracts, and poems. The selected section K endpoint is 1475; the U-M header's 1479 statement describes a broader continuation tradition, not this selection.

agents.name: Anonymous translator of the earlier English prose Brut
notes: Translator of the common English prose Brut through 1333; no broader anonymous authorship is inferred.

agents.name: Anonymous Brut continuators and compilers
notes: Collective placeholder for independently transmitted post-1333 continuations selected by Brie; component identities remain open.

agents.name: John Page
notes: Authorship is scoped only to The Siege of Rouen in component D, printed pp. 394-427.

agents.name: Friedrich W. D. Brie
notes: Editor, selector, and collator of the 1906 and 1908 volumes; not author of the medieval texts.

agents.name: Frederick J. Furnivall
notes: Editorial contributor responsible for running heads across both printed volumes.

agents.name: William Caxton
notes: Printer of the 1480 Chronicles of England; the historical TCD catalog attribution is not transferred to the anonymous manuscript text.

agents.name: Parker Library, Corpus Christi College, Cambridge

rights.basis: The selected contents were published in the 1906/1908 edition before 1929; this conclusion is limited to the selected historical text in the United States.
The selection includes transcriptions from manuscript material whose publication history may matter under UK unpublished-work rules; no blanket conclusion is made.
The pinned CME repository LICENSE is the Unlicense; this applies to the repository artifact layer, not upstream texts, witnesses, or images.
The XML header says 'Where applicable, subject to copyright' and supplies no item-specific license; negative probes do not change that conclusion.
The XML header says 'Where applicable, subject to copyright' and supplies no item-specific license; negative probes do not change that conclusion.

editorial_practices.description: Brie combines the common prose Brut through 1333 with independently transmitted continuations, extracts, appendices, and poems rather than presenting one homogeneous medieval composition.
Rawlinson B. 171 supplies Part I pp. 14-44 and 48-286, while Douce 323 supplies pp. 1-13 and 45-47; later sections have separately scoped bases and collators.
editorial_practices.scope: Part I and the separately modeled Part II relations

editorial_practices.description: The encoding follows PDF pp. 23-311 (printed 1-289) and 331-644 (printed 291-604), omitting intervening Part II paratext and absent printed page 290.
The electronic text supplies a combined title-page representation rather than reproducing all separate carrier title and paratext leaves.
Frederick J. Furnivall supplied running heads across both printed volumes; this is recorded as editorially selected paratext rather than medieval authorship.

evidence.citation: CME repository LICENSE at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Unlicense/public-domain dedication for the repository artifact layer only

evidence.citation: Pinned CME APG1531 XML header and synthetic title page.
evidence.summary: APG1531 identity; 2006 U-M publication and encoding statement; Brie edition source statement; legacy availability statement; synthetic combined title-page scope; U-M header's attributed broader-continuation claim through 1479, retained as conflict evidence rather than the selected endpoint

evidence.citation: Pinned CME APG1531 XML body, divisions, page breaks, and manuscript milestones.
evidence.summary: encoded content structure; 15 exact components on printed pages 1-289 and 291-604; printed page 290 absent; base-witness folio runs; mixed prose and verse form; section K selected endpoint in 1475
evidence.locator: BODY/DIV1/DIV2; 634 MILESTONE elements; 604 PB elements in body plus title-page verso

evidence.citation: Friedrich W. D. Brie, ed., The Brut, Part I, Preface, pp. ix-xi (1906).
evidence.summary: composite-selection boundary; anonymous early translation; Rawl. B. 171 base; Douce 323 supplies and collation; TCD 490 collation; Appendix A sources; unpublished promised third volume

evidence.citation: Friedrich W. D. Brie, ed., The Brut, Part II, Preface, pp. v-viii (1908).
evidence.summary: independent authors/times/places/intentions; selection and diplomatic editorial policy; all Part II base and collating sources; section K/Lambeth 84 collection explicitly closes in 1475; Furnivall running heads

evidence.citation: Internet Archive complete two-parts-bound-together Brie carrier and page-number map.
evidence.summary: complete 652-page carrier identity; two exact XML-to-edition page runs; intervening paratext omitted from XML; printed-page discontinuity at 290
evidence.locator: PDF pages 23-311 = printed pages 1-289; PDF pages 312-330 = Part II paratext excluded from the encoding; PDF pages 331-644 = printed pages 291-604; printed page 290 absent from the page-number map

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries catalog records and Digital Bodleian item manifests for Douce 323 and Rawlinson B. 171/173.
evidence.summary: current shelfmarks/descriptions/dates/languages; partial digital coverage; date conflict with Brie's circa-1400 statement for Rawl. B. 171

evidence.citation: J. H. Bernard and R. A. S. Macalister, Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Trinity College, Dublin (1900), MS 490, with current TCD access pages.
evidence.summary: physical witness description; historical Caxton attribution conflict; current reading-room and imaging route

evidence.citation: British Library exact manuscript catalog records for the eleven APG1531 source witnesses.
evidence.summary: exact current BL identifiers and descriptions; no linked digital surrogates in the retrieved records; physical access route

evidence.citation: Parker Library MS 174 item metadata and complete IIIF surrogate.
evidence.summary: section B witness identity and work portion; complete online access; CC BY-NC 4.0 image terms
evidence.locator: CCCC MS 174; Brut continuation at fols. 172v-198v; IIIF canvases 348-400; 404 canvases total

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library repository records and James descriptions for MSS Kk.1.12 and Hh.6.9.
evidence.summary: Kk.1.12 and Hh.6.9 identities/descriptions; Middle English Brut and Siege of Rouen contents; catalog-object CC BY 4.0 terms

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge MS O.9.1 catalog record and complete IIIF surrogate.
evidence.summary: section F witness identity and work portion; complete online access; CC BY-NC 4.0 image terms

evidence.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Lambeth Palace (1932), exact entries, and current Lambeth access pages.
evidence.summary: five exact Lambeth witnesses; section I and K base ranges; Appendix B and section H collation; current access and imaging route

evidence.citation: Catalogue of the Arundel Manuscripts in the Library of the College of Arms (1829), Arundel MS LVIII, with current repository access pages.
evidence.summary: 22-line Halidon Hill fragment; relationship to Harley 4690 version; current appointment requirements

evidence.summary: digital-image terms are distinct from medieval textual rights; provider-specific reproduction restrictions and permissions

open_questions.question: Which selected continuations, extracts, and poems should be identified as independent works rather than edition components?
next_steps: Research each component against current Brut scholarship before splitting; keep this packet's edition-level boundary meanwhile.
notes: Blocking scope from frozen packet: component-level work manifests

open_questions.question: Can any anonymous continuation be assigned more specifically and with current scholarly support?
next_steps: Consult current Brut manuscript scholarship; do not generalize John Page's authorship beyond the Siege of Rouen poem.
notes: Blocking scope from frozen packet: fine-grained authorship

open_questions.question: What does the current detailed TCD catalog record say for MS 490, and how does its modern foliation map to Brie's collation?
next_steps: Request the current record from Research Collections; Abbott 1900 is retained only as historical evidence.
notes: Blocking scope from frozen packet: current TCD dating/provenance/folio mapping

next_steps: Retry the exact catalog records or ask Lambeth; the complete James 1932 catalog and current general access pages are cached.
notes: Blocking scope from frozen packet: current item-level access assertions for MSS 6, 84, 331, and 738

open_questions.question: Is there a current detailed College of Arms catalog record or surrogate for Arundel MS LVIII fol. 334v?
next_steps: Request current catalog detail or imaging from the College of Arms; the 1829 catalog and current appointment route are cached.
notes: Blocking scope from frozen packet: current date/provenance and digital coverage

open_questions.question: Which Latin passages are intrinsic components versus editorial/table material?
next_steps: Review the 15 tables and mixed-language passages at component level before assigning Latin beyond a scoped secondary language.
notes: Blocking scope from frozen packet: component-level language proportions

open_questions.question: Is ISBN 9780859918794 a photographic reprint of both original volumes without substantive revision?
next_steps: Inspect the current physical/digital reprint before adding a reprints or version relation; for now it is only an access option.
notes: Blocking scope from frozen packet: reprint lineage relation

review_notes: Cataloging boundary: an edition-level selected compilation, not one homogeneous medieval work and not the complete contents of any witness.
The selected and encoded section K endpoint is 1475. The U-M header's 1479 statement is retained only as an attributed broader-tradition conflict and is not projected onto APG1531.
No copied_from edge is asserted because no raw U-M production package was recovered; repository artifact, U-M encoding, Brie edition, selected historical text, and IA carrier remain separate layers.
All 64 frozen cache paths are projected through the access routes that actually deliver them. Repeated access attachments are intentional; unique cached bytes are counted once: 513592589 bytes.
Witness and facsimile entity cores contain only intrinsic facts; APG-specific loci and selection boundaries remain in scoped relations and access records.
